steps.title: Identify the grammar issue
steps.content: The subject is even-toed ungulates (plural). The phrase "such as the domestic goat" is a parenthetical example — strip it out and the sentence reads: "even-toed ungulates ______ to a vast and diverse order of animals." A plural subject needs a plural verb.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A: belongs — third-person singular (-s ending). "Even-toed ungulates belongs" — subject-verb mismatch. Eliminated.

B: has belonged — singular auxiliary "has." Same number error. Eliminated.

C: is belonging — singular auxiliary "is." Another number error, plus progressive aspect is unidiomatic for a fixed taxonomic fact. Eliminated.

D: belong — plural base form. "Even-toed ungulates belong to a vast and diverse order" — subject and verb agree. Correct.
